title: CAP Theorem in Five Minutes
description: A senior asks you to explain CAP theorem to a non-technical PM. Walk the trade-offs without jargon overload.

t1.text: Hey, the new PM is joining tomorrow. Can you explain CAP theorem to her without scaring her off?

templates.text: Sure. The simple version: in a distributed database, you can only get two out of three guarantees.
templates.hint_zh: 好,簡單版:分散式資料庫只能同時保住三個保證裡的兩個。

templates.text: Consistency, Availability, and Partition tolerance. Network partitions are unavoidable, so you really pick between C and A.
templates.hint_zh: 一致性、可用性、分區容錯。網路分區不可避免,所以實質是在 C 跟 A 之間二選一。

t5.text: Got it. Give me a concrete example of each side.

templates.text: A bank ledger picks CP, so it stops serving rather than show wrong balances. A social feed picks AP, so it stays up but might show stale posts.
templates.hint_zh: 銀行帳本選 CP,寧可停服務也不顯示錯誤餘額;社群動態選 AP,寧可顯示舊資料也要持續可用。

t7.text: Where does eventual consistency fit in?

templates.text: It's the practical middle ground. Most real systems aren't pure CP or AP. They sync up after a delay.
templates.hint_zh: 務實的中間值,大多數現實系統不是純 CP 或純 AP,而是稍後同步補上。

t9.text: One more thing — the C in CAP and the C in ACID. Same thing?

templates.text: Different beasts. CAP's C is about nodes agreeing. ACID's C is about a transaction leaving the database in a valid state.
templates.hint_zh: 不一樣。CAP 的 C 講節點之間達成共識,ACID 的 C 講交易結束後資料庫仍處於有效狀態。

t11.text: Perfect. That's exactly the level I wanted. Mind sending her a one-pager before tomorrow?

templates.text: Will do. I'll keep it under a page and link the deeper docs.
templates.hint_zh: 沒問題,我會控制在一頁內,深入的文件用連結帶。
